title: HEK293 maintenance (ATCC)
description: Adherent HEK293 (ATCC CRL-1573) in EMEM + 10% FBS, split 1:6 to 1:10. Source: ATCC https://www.atcc.org/products/crl-1573

payload.description: Routine maintenance of adherent HEK293 cells per the ATCC CRL-1573 product sheet. ATCC complete growth medium is EMEM (ATCC 30-2003) + 10% FBS (many labs substitute DMEM, but EMEM is the ATCC-recommended base). Subcultivation ratio 1:6 to 1:10. Seed at 1 x 10^4 to 4 x 10^4 viable cells/cm^2 and subculture before cells exceed 6 to 7 x 10^4 cells/cm^2. Doubling time is not published on the ATCC product sheet, so it is omitted. The day cadence below is an editable starting point; adjust it so the next passage lands near confluence. Source: ATCC CRL-1573 https://www.atcc.org/products/crl-1573

cell_line.name: HEK293
cell_line.notes: ATCC CRL-1573. Grow at 37 C, 5% CO2. Serum is fetal bovine serum (ATCC 30-2020) at 10%. Source: https://www.atcc.org/products/crl-1573

planned_events.notes: Seed day. Rinse the monolayer, add 2 to 3 mL Trypsin-EDTA (ATCC 30-2101), disperse 5 to 15 min at 37 C, quench with complete medium, count, and reseed at 1 x 10^4 to 4 x 10^4 cells/cm^2 into a fresh T75.
Confirm even attachment, healthy morphology, and no contamination.
Subculture near confluence (ATCC: before 6 to 7 x 10^4 cells/cm^2). Repeat the trypsinize, quench, and reseed.
